As of the knowledge cutoff (May 5, 2025), there is no evidence that the People's Republic of China has launched a ground invasion of Taiwan. Although China conducted large-scale military drills ("Strait Thunder-2025A") blockading and simulating strikes on Taiwan on April 1, 2025, analysts noted that China does not yet have the capability for a full-scale invasion, and these exercises were widely characterized as intimidation tactics. An amphibious invasion of Taiwan would require massive, highly visible military buildup and logistical preparation spanning months, which has not been reported. With less than two months remaining until the July 1, 2025 resolution date, the likelihood of China mobilizing and initiating an unprovoked ground invasion that culminates in an official U.S. accusation within this 8-week window is extraordinarily low. Given system constraints bounding the forecast between 0.05 and 0.95, I am registering the lowest allowed probability.
